name: Toast

variants.name: critical
variants.description: An issue has occurred that prevents further processing.

variants.name: warning
variants.description: A problem or inconsistency has arisen. Users can carry on working.

variants.name: success
variants.description: An action has been performed without errors or warnings.

headline.note: Short, specific, sentence case. Avoid articles like the, a, an.

actions.note: When actions are shown, toast persists until dismissed. Action labels must be descriptive — not OK or Cancel.

auto_dismiss_ms.note: Auto-dismisses after 5s if cursor is outside. Ignored when actions are present.

behaviour.entry: Slides in from the right of the screen
behaviour.stacking: Multiple toasts stack vertically, newest on top
behaviour.auto_dismiss: 5 seconds if cursor outside toast. Paused when cursor is over toast.
behaviour.review: After dismissal, user can review in notification menu

sizing.width_small: Stretches to available width minus margin for layout S

usage_rules.rule: Do not use toast for critical interactions requiring immediate action
usage_rules.detail: If the user must immediately respond to a critical message, use Alert dialog instead. Toasts are ephemeral and auto-dismiss.
Toasts are for non-disruptive feedback not necessarily related to page content. Do not use for persistent or blocking messages.
Only use the four provided variants with their defined icon and color combinations.

usage_rules.rule: Icon and color must match variant
usage_rules.detail: Always pair the correct semantic icon and color for the variant. Do not mix.
Action button labels must be descriptive (e.g. Delete, Keep). Never use OK, Cancel, Yes, No.
Avoid unnecessary articles (the, a, an) in the toast headline.

usage_rules.rule: Newest toast on top when stacking
usage_rules.detail: When multiple toasts are shown, stack vertically with the newest one on top.

accessibility.aria_live: assertive for critical, polite for others
accessibility.auto_dismiss_note: Do not auto-dismiss if actions are present — user must be able to interact

confidence_blockers: If ticket uses toast for a critical blocking action requiring immediate response, flag and suggest Alert dialog.
If action labels are OK, Cancel, Yes, or No, flag as usage_rule violation.
If ticket requests custom colors or icons in toast, flag.

embedding_text: Toast notification component. Ephemeral status update slides from right. Variants: critical warning success information. Auto-dismisses 5 seconds. Persists when actions shown. Stacks newest on top. Not for critical blocking interactions use Alert dialog instead. No custom colors or icons. Descriptive action labels. Sentence case headline no articles.
